title: Dev Home
summary: Repo-wide development workflow, testing, environment, and deployment references.
content_html:
Dev Home

Repository Shape

  • site/ is the checked public GitHub Pages artifact. There is no deploy-time copy/build step.
  • Site owns the public catalogue, route, shell, design, and runtime documentation.
  • Studio edits catalogue/editorial source and builds public catalogue payloads.
  • Analytics owns canonical tag workflows and hosts Data Sharing.
  • Docs Viewer renders/manages document scopes; public routes reuse site-owned assets/config.
  • Admin runs/reviews operational audits, checks, activity, and test summaries.
  • Processing owns repository hosting and integration for the separate Java/Processing project; its own Docs Viewer scope retains conceptual and technical documentation.

Work Lifecycle

  • Development Workflow — concept, architecture, roadmap, delivery, verification, documentation, closeout.
  • Development Checklist — cross-cutting implementation guardrails.
  • Roadmap — app roadmaps, feature direction, and bounded deliveries.
  • Testing and Run Checks — select evidence and profiles.

Structure And Environment

  • Architecture — cross-app structure and ownership.
  • Configuration Map — find checked config/registry owners.
  • Source Tree Ownership — public, local-app, shared, generated, and output roots.
  • Local Setup — local runners/environment.
  • Runtime Dependencies — dependency sources and workflow-specific tools.
  • Cloud Environments — .codex/Codespaces bootstrap.
  • GitHub Actions — current public-site validation/deploy workflow.

Documentation Shape

Studio-scope Markdown is flat under docs-viewer/scopes/studio/source/; parent_id builds the navigation tree. Treat code/config/tests as exact authority and these docs as maps to capabilities, methodology, extension points, and known gaps.
